title: Mock Production Dashboard
tagline: Interactive production KPI dashboard with fabricated 30-day data, live charts, and auto-generated recommendations.
objective: Design and build a self-contained, interactive production KPI dashboard — using fabricated but realistic data — to demonstrate applied web development and data analysis skills alongside the hands-on manufacturing projects on this site.
challenges: Building custom line, bar, and scatter charts from scratch in vanilla JavaScript and SVG, with no external charting libraries, while keeping the visualizations readable and interactive.
Generating a fabricated 30-day dataset that behaves like real production data, including realistic noise, trends, and anomalies worth flagging.
Computing recommendations directly from the underlying data (spikes, dips, week-over-week trends, day-of-week patterns) rather than hardcoding them, so the insights are genuinely data-driven.
outcomes: Delivered a fully interactive dashboard with hover tooltips, a 2x2 chart grid, and an auto-ranked Recommendations panel, styled to match the rest of this site.
Demonstrates the same applied-AI and data-analysis skillset used to build the Power BI dashboards referenced in the Experience section, translated into a live, clickable web format.
Structured so new KPIs, charts, or a real data source can be swapped in without touching layout code.
note: Fabricated data — for demonstration purposes only.

title: Horseman's Axe — SFSA Cast in Steel 2026
tagline: Competition-ready, historically accurate cast steel axe built to strict weight, length, and porosity requirements.
objective: Design and manufacture a historically accurate, competition-ready Horseman's Axe for the SFSA Cast In Steel 2026 competition, satisfying strict weight (≤ 3.3 lb.), length (≤ 31.5 in.), and porosity (≤ 10%) requirements while demonstrating casting creativity, authenticity, and quality control.
challenges: Balancing historical authenticity with manufacturability by selecting manufacturing processes that ensure dimensional accuracy while remaining cost-effective for production.
Achieving target hardness and toughness through strategic material selection and heat treatment.
Coordinating long foundry lead times and multiple casting processes across vendors, requiring proactive contingency planning to keep the project on schedule.
outcomes: Delivered a final assembly that met all customer requirements, validated through a 20-unit production run, alongside a comprehensive technical report covering budget, schedule, part drawings, simulation results, FMEA, routing sheets, testing procedures, and project video.
Competed against 58 teams from 36 schools; passed all five rounds of performance testing.
Built hands-on experience in design for manufacturing, process selection, FEA/casting simulation, and cross-functional project management.

title: Production Dogsled Redesign
tagline: Redesigned a legacy all-steel shop transport fixture into a lighter, foldable aluminum structure.
objective: Redesign production dogsleds used to transport sheet metal channels between departments, replacing the legacy all-steel design with an aluminum structure. Key requirements included reducing overall weight, incorporating a folding mechanism to enable stackable storage, and designing welding fixtures to support repeatable, manufacturable production.
challenges: Engineering a structural design that leveraged aluminum's strength-to-weight ratio while minimizing material usage, and selecting casters and hinges that met performance requirements while remaining cost-effective for production across multiple units.
Balanced functional design requirements with aesthetic considerations, ensuring the dogsleds were visually presentable for use by the marketing team to transport parts to trade shows and displays.
outcomes: Delivered a redesigned dogsled that met all customer requirements, achieving an approximate 50% reduction in weight compared to the original steel design.
The folding/stacking functionality performed as intended, reducing the facility's floor space footprint previously occupied by the sleds.

images.alt: Finished aluminum production dogsleds, folded and stacked
